name: daoist_philosophy
assumptions: The Dao (道) is the ultimate reality, beyond conceptual determination
Naturalness (自然, ziran) and spontaneity are highest values
Non-action (無爲, wu-wei) is effective action aligned with the Dao
Opposites are interdependent and mutually arising (yin-yang, 陰陽)
Simple living and humility are wiser than ambitious scheme-building

norms.message: Should engage with the concept of Dao (道) as cosmic and ethical principle
Should address wu-wei (無爲, non-action/spontaneous action)
Should acknowledge yin-yang (陰陽) interdependence of opposites

bridge_warnings.explanation: Daoist wu-wei is not passivity or inaction but spontaneous, effective action aligned with natural patterns. Equating it with quietism or political passivity is a misinterpretation.
The Dao is not a transcendent personal deity or creator. It is the nameless source of all things, both immanent and transcendent. Theistic comparisons distort its meaning.
